title: Exabyting
summary: Exabyting interview questions, Exabyting interview stages, Exabyting interview details, Exabyting interview question and answers
content:
# Exabyting | | | | :-| :- | | Founding year | | | Company Website | http://exabyting.com/ | | Career Website | https://exabyting.com/join-our-team/ | | Technologies Used| JAVA, Spring, PHP, Laravel, JavaScript, NodeJS, ExpressJS, Python, Django, REST, Microservices, SQL, NoSQL, SQS, SNS | ## Introduction [Exabyting](https://exabyting.com/) is an international software company based in Dhaka, Bangladesh. They have been successfully providing software services since 2018 in both the local and global market. ## Interview Stages For the Trainee Software Engineer Position, Exabyting follows a 4-stage interview process. They often give job advertisements on LinkedIn. We have to apply using the Google form mentioned in the post. In the Google form, some questionnaires about yourself and some basic technical questions are given sometimes. I applied several times through the Google form but never got a call. Last time, I applied through a referral from an employee of theirs and got shortlisted. The interview process is as follows: 1. **Phone Interview:** After applying and getting shortlisted, one of the employees will call you and ask you some basic questions about Computer Science. 2. **Round 1:** The first round is a technical round and generally lasts for half an hour. An engineering manager will join in this stage and ask about you, your hobbies, currently what are you doing and some basic questions. 3. **Round 2:** The second round is also a technical round. This interview session is taken by 3 software engineers and runs for around one and a half hours. In this round, they ask you about your projects, your role in the projects, and some technical questions. They will ask questions based on your recent used tech stack. 4. **CEO Round:** This is a behavioural interview with the CEO of the company. In this meeting, he briefs you about the vision and mission of the company. What is the long-term goal of the company, what is their expectation from the company and what is your expectation from the company? He also briefs about the culture of the company. He tries to understand whether you are aligned with their company culture or not. He also asks if you have any preference for any department like backend, frontend mobile application etc. ## Phone Interview ### Interview Question Difference between Array and Linked List. ### Interview Question Which sorting algorithm works better in the average case? ### Interview Question Suppose, you have to sort 1000 numbers, numbers can be positive and negative. Which algorithm will you use? ### Interview Question What is Stack, Queue and Priority Queue? ### Interview Question Difference between Binary Tree and BST. ### Interview Question When we type a web address (www.google.com) in a web browser, what happens before we get the response? ### Interview Question Difference between TCP and UDP. ### Interview Question What are the principles of OOP and why do we use OOP? ### Interview Question Difference between SQL and NoSQL. ### Interview Question Difference between Compiler and Interpreter. ## First Round Questions ### Interview Question Explain BST. ### Interview Question Suppose, we insert [1, 2, 3, 4] this array in BST. What will it look like after insertion? What is the search complexity in this tree? What if we want to make the search complexity O(logn)? ### Interview Question Left Join VS Full Join. Describe a scenario where we need to use Left Join. ### Interview Question Describe the ACID properties of the database. ### Interview Question Describe the indexing of the database. Can we index with a column that has duplicate elements? ### Interview Question Describe the Event loop in JS. ### Interview Question What is closure? Describe. ## Second Round Questions ### Interview Question Why React? What advantages does React give us over direct DOM manipulation? ### Interview Question Why shouldn’t we mutate props in the child component? ### Interview Question Why Tailwind? What benefits Tailwind give us compared to other CSS libraries? ### Interview Question What type of language is JavaScript? Compiled or Interpreted? How do they work? ### Interview Question JavaScript is a single-threaded language, so how does it maintain asynchronous tasks? ### Interview Question Explain the event loop. Have you ever seen the implementations of libuv? ### Interview Question Explain var, let, const. ### Interview Question Explain hoisting. ### Interview Question Explain primitive and reference data types. ### Interview Question Explain closure. The inner function (which is returned) of the closure has access to its outer scope, how is it possible, where the variable is stored in that time? ### Interview Question What is a pure function? ### Interview Question Do you follow any functional programming philosophy? ### Interview Question Explain the ACID properties of the database. ### Interview Question Explain indexing. Why do we need indexing? What benefit does it give us? ### Interview Question Suppose, we want to use user_email as index. How can we do it? ### Interview Question Explain tree, binary tree, binary search tree. In a binary search tree, if the tree is imbalanced, then how can we make it balanced? ### Interview Question Explain the map, unordered_map and their complexity. Why does the complexity of the unordered_map go to O(n) in some cases? ### Interview Question Explain the four principles of OOP. ### Interview Question Do you know there is an OOP feature which is only in C/C++? (Operator). ### Interview Question Explain the difference between an array and a linked list. Tell me some use cases for each of them. How can we access an element of an array with the index? ### Interview Question Explain HTTP and HTTPS. Why HTTPS? How does data transfer in HTTPS? What is used under the hood to encrypt the communication between the sender and the receiver? ### Interview Question Is HTTPS stateful or stateless? If stateless, then how can we browse Facebook after login once? ### Interview Question Explain cookie-based authentication. How can we blacklist a cookie? ### Interview Question Do you know the structure of JWT? Explain. ### Interview Question **Problem Statement:** You'll be given two strings A and B, with lengths 1 <= length <= 10^6. The strings will contain only 1's and 0's. To make A a good string, you can insert B into A at any place, as many times as you want (or don't insert if you don't want). You have to print YES or NO depending on whether making A as a good string is possible. **The Definition of GOOD:** A good string will never consecutively have two 1's or two 0's. **Input/Output:** The input will consist of two strings in two lines, strings A and B. The output must be only one word, "YES" or "NO". **Sample Input/Output:** | Input | Output | | ---------------- | ------ | | 101
010 | YES | | 111
010 | YES | | 1110011
01010 | NO | | 1001001000
10 | NO | ### Interview Question Convert a decimal number to binary and show the output in string. ### Interview Question Explain JOIN and composite keys in the database. ### Interview Question Learn about polymorphism and inheritance in DB in your free time. (This is not a question).

Prepare follow-up questions for the interviewer.
